skill.name: python-sdk
skill.description: Python SDK for inference.sh - run AI apps, build agents, and integrate with 150+ models. Package: inferencesh (pip install inferencesh). Supports sync/async, streaming, file uploads. Build agents with template or ad-hoc patterns, tool builder API, skills, and human approval. Use for: Python integration, AI apps, agent development, RAG pipelines, automation. Triggers: python sdk, inferencesh, pip install, python api, python client, async inference, python agent, tool builder python, programmatic ai, python integration, sdk python
skill.summary: Python SDK for inference.sh - run AI apps, build agents, and integrate with 150+ models
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 357 potential issues, but evaluation reveals these are largely false positives. The 'external_commands' findings (220+) are markdown code fences being misinterpreted. The 'blocker' findings for weak crypto and system reconnaissance are also false positives. Network, filesystem, and env_access patterns are standard SDK functionality. One documentation pattern shows eval() usage in tool-builder.md which is a code example, not executable. This is a legitimate Python SDK for an AI inference platform.

medium_findings.title: Documentation shows eval() usage pattern
medium_findings.description: The tool-builder.md documentation includes a code example demonstrating a calculator tool that uses eval() on user-provided expressions. This is a documentation pattern, not executable code, but could be misused if copied directly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: This is a documentation example showing how to build a tool, not actual malicious code. The eval() is intentionally shown as a pattern demonstration.

low_findings.title: Standard SDK network access
low_findings.description: The SDK makes network calls to inference.sh API for AI inference. This is expected functionality for an AI SDK.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network calls to inference.sh API are core functionality of this AI inference SDK.

low_findings.title: Standard file operations for AI SDK
low_findings.description: The SDK uses standard Python file operations (os.path.getsize, os.unlink) for file handling in AI workflows.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: File operations are expected for an AI SDK that handles file uploads and processing.

low_findings.title: API key via environment variable
low_findings.description: Documentation shows using os.environ for API key access, which is the recommended secure practice.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Using environment variables for API keys is the recommended secure practice.

content.user_title: Build AI apps with Python SDK
content.value_statement: Integrate 150+ AI models into Python applications. The SDK provides simple APIs for running AI apps, building agents, handling async operations, and managing file uploads without complex setup.
actual_capabilities: Run AI apps with simple client.run() calls
Build multi-agent systems with tool orchestration
Handle async operations for long-running tasks
Upload and process files with AI models
Create custom tools with the tool builder API
limitations: Requires inference.sh API key for authentication
Internet connection required for AI inference
Rate limits apply based on API tier
Models are hosted by inference.sh, not local

use_cases.title: Python developer building AI features
use_cases.description: Add image generation, text analysis, or other AI capabilities to Python applications with minimal code changes.
use_cases.target_user: Software developers integrating AI into existing Python projects

use_cases.title: Data scientist creating AI pipelines
use_cases.description: Build automated workflows that process data through multiple AI models in sequence.
use_cases.target_user: Data scientists building ML pipelines and automation

use_cases.title: Developer building AI agents
use_cases.description: Create autonomous agents that can use tools, make decisions, and handle complex multi-step tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Basic AI app execution
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the python-sdk skill to run the flux-schnell app with the prompt 'A sunset over mountains'. Show me the output.
prompt_templates.scenario: Running a simple AI image generation task

prompt_templates.title: Async task with polling
prompt_templates.prompt: Start a video generation task using python-sdk with the 'google/veo-3-1-fast' app. Don't wait for completion. Show me the task ID so I can check status later.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a long-running AI task without waiting

prompt_templates.title: Building an agent with tools
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a python-sdk agent that has access to a web search tool and a calculator tool. Show how the agent would use both tools to answer a complex question.

prompt_templates.title: Streaming response handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to use python-sdk to stream responses from an LLM and display progress updates as they arrive.

output_examples.output: Task completed successfully. Generated image available at: https://cloud.inference.sh/outputs/abc123.png
Agent created with 3 tools: search_web, calculator, file_reader. The agent can now autonomously decide which tool to use based on user requests.

best_practices: Store API keys in environment variables, never hardcode them
Use async mode for long-running tasks to avoid blocking
Implement retry logic with exponential backoff for reliability
anti_patterns: Hardcoding API keys in source code or configuration files
Blocking on long-running tasks without using async patterns
Not handling rate limits or API errors gracefully

faq.question: How do I get an API key?
faq.answer: Sign up at inference.sh, go to Settings, then API Keys, and create a new key. Keep it secure and never share it publicly.

faq.question: Can I use this with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill is designed to work with Claude Code, Codex, and Claude. It provides Python integration capabilities for AI agents.

faq.question: What models are available?
faq.answer: The SDK supports 150+ models including image generators (Flux, DALL-E), video generators (Veo), and language models (Claude, GPT, Gemini).

faq.question: How does pricing work?
faq.answer: API usage is billed by inference.sh based on model type and compute time. Check their pricing page for current rates.

faq.question: Can I run models locally?
faq.answer: No, this SDK connects to inference.sh's cloud platform. For local inference, consider libraries like Ollama or llama.cpp.

faq.question: Is this secure?
faq.answer: Yes, the SDK uses HTTPS for all connections and recommends storing API keys in environment variables. Review the security best practices in the documentation.
